text:
Market today: Mood is cautiously mixed. Micron's blowout earnings are the real driver today - memory/chip stocks getting a lift, which helps your chip-heavy book. But Apple dropping 5% on price hikes from the memory crunch is a drag on broader tech sentiment, and Iran's Strait of Hormuz threat adds macro risk.

Your holdings: Three need attention. PLTR is down -16.7% and flagged SELL - on margin, that hole is already painful and getting worse. AVGO is your largest position, down -13.3% and flagged TRIM - size plus loss on margin is a real problem if it slides further toward the $336 stop. SPAL is down -17.1% - this is a 2x leveraged ETF that decays daily, losses accelerate fast, and margin amplifies that further. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with limited price history - treat it as fragile.

Watch: 1) How AVGO trades today given the Micron/memory read-through. 2) Whether the Iran Hormuz headline escalates and hits risk sentiment broadly.

Bottom line: Get your arms around PLTR, AVGO, and SPAL before margin turns a manageable drawdown into a forced liquidation.
